title: Tesla targets 10M Optimus robots — currently folding laundry
desc: Daily AI news satire. Humanoid robot factories ramping; the early units fold one shirt at a time. Wall Street pegs the market at $13B by 2029.

beats.name: factory-horizon-cold-open
beats.narration: The long-promised humanoid-robot factories are finally coming online.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on the factory horizon (1.0x -> 1.04x)

beats.name: musk-surveys-robot-legion
beats.narration: Tesla has begun Optimus production at its Fremont plant this quarter.
beats.cinematography: Static — let Musk's surveying-the-army pose carry it

beats.name: optimus-production-target
beats.narration: With a long-term target of ten million units a year out of Giga Texas.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-out on the production-target counter as it ticks (1.04x -> 1.0x)

beats.name: adcock-with-figure-prototype
beats.narration: Across the country, Figure AI is now valued at thirty-nine billion dollars.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on Adcock's prototype orb (1.0x -> 1.04x)

beats.name: funding-scroll-unfurls
beats.narration: Apptronik has raised five hundred and twenty million at a five-billion valuation. One X is shipping its NEO model to home-beta households.
beats.cinematography: Subtle drift on the unfurling scroll (handles itself)

beats.name: legion-assembly-cinematic
beats.cinematography: Slow hero pan across the assembled legion (1.0x -> 1.05x over full beat)

beats.name: SMASH-CUT-suburban-kitchen
beats.cinematography: **SMASH CUT from beat 06. No fade. Brief 0.1s magenta flash on the cut.**

beats.name: robot-folds-one-tshirt-slow
beats.narration: The robots execute the tasks for which they were designed.
beats.cinematography: Static (the slow-folding motion IS the gag)

beats.name: robot-loads-one-fork
beats.cinematography: Static (the slow-loading motion IS the gag)

beats.name: robot-stares-at-staircase
beats.cinematography: Static, extra dwell — robot motionless at the bottom of the stairs

beats.name: analyst-deadpan-headset
beats.narration: Wall Street pegs the humanoid market at roughly thirteen billion dollars by twenty-twenty-nine.
beats.cinematography: Subtle drift — analyst's side-eye carries it

beats.name: musk-adcock-still-promising-split + robot-still-folding
beats.narration: The founders remain confident. The robot remains folding.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on the still-folding robot in evening light (1.0x -> 1.06x over full 7s)

sources.attribution: Tesla targets 10M Optimus units with new Texas plant
Apptronik raises $520 million at $5 billion valuation for Apollo robot
Top 10 Best Humanoid Robot Manufacturers in 2026 (Figure AI $39B valuation; 1X NEO + Figure 03 home-beta status)
Elon Musk sets 2026 Optimus sale date — Where other humanoid robots stand
Tesla robot price in 2026 — Everything you need to know about Optimus

transcript: The long-promised humanoid-robot factories are finally coming online. Tesla has begun Optimus production at its Fremont plant this quarter. With a long-term target of ten million units a year out of Giga Texas. Across the country, Figure AI is now valued at thirty-nine billion dollars. Apptronik has raised five hundred and twenty million at a five-billion valuation. One X is shipping its NEO model to home-beta households. The legion is finally assembled. Meanwhile in early-access households. The robots execute the tasks for which they were designed. Slowly. Very slowly. Wall Street pegs the humanoid market at roughly thirteen billion dollars by twenty-twenty-nine. The founders remain confident. The robot remains folding.

writing.author: Claude
writing.role: script · beat plan · narration · sources audit
